programme.title: Hudson & Rex

programme.title: Killshot

programme.title: Reasonable Doubt
programme.desc: A DA's life is turned around when he's involved in a hit and run and another man is arrested for his crime.

programme.title: The Frozen Ground
programme.desc: A cop partners with a young woman who escaped the clutches of a serial killer to bring the murderer to justice

programme.title: Desperation Road
programme.desc: A woman and her daughter are caught in the crossfire when whiskey, guns, and revenge violently intersect.

programme.title: Silent Night
programme.desc: A tormented father seeks vengeance after his son dies in a gang’s crossfire.

programme.title: Before I Go To Sleep [Broadcast Edit]
programme.desc: Ever since a vicious attack nearly claimed her life, Christine Lucas (Nicole Kidman) has suffered from anterograde amnesia and is unable to form new memories. Every morning, she becomes reacquainted with her husband, Ben (Colin Firth), and the other constants in her life. In accordance with her doctor's (Mark Strong) instructions, Christine keeps a video diary. As Christine starts to uncover terrifying truths about her past, she begins to question everything -- and everyone -- around her.

programme.title: My Mom's New Boyfriend
programme.desc: After several years away, FBI agent Henry Durand (Colin Hanks) returns to find his mother, Marty (Meg Ryan), a much different person: Having long mourned for Henry's father, she is now embracing single life. One day, while Marty, Henry and his new fiancée, Emily (Selma Blair), are out for a walk, they meet Tommy (Antonio Banderas), a mysterious stranger, with whom Marty then becomes involved. However, through his FBI contacts, Henry learns that Tommy is suspected of being an art thief.

programme.title: Homeboy [Broadcast Edit]
programme.desc: Mickey Rourke delivers one of his strongest performances as Johnny Walker, a washed-up boxer looking for one more shot in the ring.

programme.title: Direct Contact
programme.desc: An American prisoner in a Russian jail is given a chance at freedom if he can rescue a kidnapped woman.

programme.title: Animal Factory [Broadcast Edit]
programme.desc: Suburbanite Ron (Edward Furlong) is spoiled, young and not overly worried about the marijuana charges leveled against him. But, after being made out to be a drug dealer, he faces a five-year jail sentence in San Quentin State Prison. Physically frail and unaccustomed to his rough surroundings, Ron is primed to fall victim to sexual predators and bullying guards -- that is, until he's befriended by Earl (Willem Dafoe), a veteran inmate who finds meaning in protecting the vulnerable new kid.

programme.title: Half Nelson
programme.desc: An inner-city junior high school teacher with a drug habit forms an unlikely friendship with one of his students

programme.title: Hoop Dreams
programme.desc: Every school day, African-American teenagers William Gates and Arthur Agee travel 90 minutes each way from inner-city Chicago to St. Joseph High School in Westchester, Illinois, a predominately white suburban school well-known for the excellence of its basketball program. Gates and Agee dream of NBA stardom, and with the support of their close-knit families, they battle the social and physical obstacles that stand in their way. This acclaimed documentary was shot over the course of five years.

programme.title: Marshall
programme.desc: Thurgood Marshall, the first African-American Supreme Court Justice, battles through one of his career-defining cases.

programme.title: Darkness Falls
programme.desc: A young man, considered crazy by everyone in town, except for his childhood girlfriend and her little brother, is put to the ultimate test when he becomes the only thing that stands between a legendary evil and the little brother.

programme.title: Red Water
programme.desc: A freshwater shark wreaks havoc as it heads up a Louisiana river.

programme.title: Lake Placid Vs. Anaconda
programme.desc: When they're not chowing down on sorority girls, get ready for the heart-pounding head to head battle between the crocodiles and anacondas!

programme.title: Robot And Frank
programme.desc: Set in the near future, Robot and Frank is an offbeat buddy movie about an elderly ex-jewel thief and his new caretaker robot.

programme.title: Made Of Honor
programme.desc: Tom leads a good life: he’s sexy, he’s successful, and he knows he can always rely on Hannah, his delightful best friend and the one constant in his life. It’s the perfect setup until Hannah goes overseas to Scotland on a six-week business trip… and Tom is stunned to realize how empty his life is without her. He resolves that upon her return, he ll ask Hannah to marry him – but is floored when he learns that she has become engaged to a handsome and wealthy Scotsman and plans to move overseas. When Hannah asks Tom to be her "maid" of honor, he reluctantly agrees to fill the role… but only so he can attempt to woo Hannah and stop the wedding before it’s too late.

programme.title: Dead Reckoning
programme.desc: A decorated war veteran becomes entangled in the search for a fellow Medal of Honor winner's killer.

programme.title: Berserk!
programme.desc: The sexy owner of a small traveling circus is under suspicion when the circus becomes plagued by a spate of murders.

programme.title: The Three Stooges In Orbit
programme.desc: The Three Stooges are tangling with Martians.

programme.title: Anatomy Of A Murder
programme.desc: When his flirtatious young wife (Lee Remick) claims she was raped, an army lieutenant (Ben Gazzara)kills her attacker and hires small-town lawyer James Stewart to defend him.

programme.title: Some Girl
programme.desc: Four unstable twenty-something women search for long-term relationships in 1990s Los Angeles.

programme.title: City Hunter [Dubbed]
programme.desc: Jackie Chan stars as P.I. Ryu Saeba, who's been hired to find the runaway daughter of a publishing tycoon. Ryu's first impulse is to turn the job downâ€¦until he sees a picture of the beautiful girl, Shizuko.

programme.title: Silent House
programme.desc: When Sarah finds herself sealed inside her family's secluded lake house with no contact to the outside world, panic soon turns to terror as events become increasingly ominous.

programme.title: Rough Magic
programme.desc: Set in the 1950s, Rough Magic tells the story of what happens when a pretty apprentice magician goes to Mexico to escape her fiancé, a wealthy politician, and to find a Mayan shaman who will teach her ancient principles of magic. She is being trailed by a detective hired by her fiancé. He's a former photojournalist traumatized by what he saw in Hiroshima. The photojournalist joins her in the search for the Mayan shaman, and falls in love with her; the feeling is not reciprocated. When she finds the shaman, she drinks a potion which empowers her to do magic. The potion has life-changing effects on her and her relationship with her companion. They have strange experiences which are brought about by magic.

programme.title: Rock 'N' Roll High School
programme.desc: A group of rock-music-loving students, with the help of the Ramones, take over their school to combat its newly installed oppressive administration.

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: A disgraced cop finds himself in a race to find a kidnap victim whose abductor he accidentally killed.

programme.title: Fire with Fire
programme.desc: When the US Marshalls cannot protect Jeremy or his love from a crime lord, he stops hiding, and starts hunting

programme.title: Force of Nature
programme.desc: A gang of thieves plan a heist during a hurricane, but a cop tries to force everyone inside to evacuate.

programme.title: Freelancers
programme.desc: When Malo joins the NYPD, he is taken in by his murdered father’s old partner and a team of rogue cops.

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: A disgraced cop finds himself in a race to find a kidnap victim whose abductor he accidentally killed.

programme.title: 12 To Midnight
programme.desc: A detective gets kicked off the force when the murder of his wife drives him to drink. When a new string of murders seem connected to her death, he soon finds himself squaring off against a killer who appears to be superhuman.

programme.title: Doc Holliday 2
programme.desc: After the OK Coral shootout in Tombstone, Doc Holliday opens a small Faro Saloon in a remote town where he encounters Edward Bailey. When a Faro game goes wrong for Edward, he turns up missing and Doc Holliday finds himself on trial for his murder.

programme.title: Doc Holliday 3
programme.desc: Doc Holliday retires to Colorado after a long battle with tuberculosis and finds himself in one final adventure to save a young man from ruthless outlaws.

programme.title: Andromeda: 1883
programme.desc: Space marine Casey Reynolds travels into the past to stop a killer android bent on wiping out the one person crucial to saving the human race.

programme.title: Taken From Rio Bravo
programme.desc: Two friends pursue a group of human traffickers that have kidnapped five women in 1874, East Texas.

programme.title: The Legend of Tomiris
programme.desc: The life story of Tomiris, the great queen of the steppe, who was destined to become a skillful warrior and unite the Scythian and Saka tribes.

programme.title: Baby Assassins
programme.desc: Two high school girls who are about to graduate also happen to be highly-skilled assassins. When the organization that employs them orders them to share a room, their relationship turns sour.

programme.title: Baby Assassins 2
programme.desc: Chisato and Mahiro must pay four years of overdue fees or lose their membership in their assassin guild. They take part-time jobs, but find themselves in the middle of an armed robbery and being hunted by other assassins.

programme.title: Furie
programme.desc: A former gang leader battles human traffickers to save her kidnapped daughter.

programme.title: Mom, Dad and Her
programme.desc: Sydney Foster is sent to spend the summer with her father.

programme.title: Guilty at 17
programme.desc: Traci is lured into claiming she saw a teacher sexually harass a student.

programme.title: I Know What I Saw
programme.desc: A woman MacKenzie struggling with the suicide of her father.

programme.title: My Life is a Lifetime Movie
programme.desc: Shortly after earning her degree at Northern Kentucky University.

programme.title: Mom, Dad and Her
programme.desc: Sydney Foster is sent to spend the summer with her father.

programme.title: Guilty at 17
programme.desc: Traci is lured into claiming she saw a teacher sexually harass a student.

programme.title: A Royal Recipe for Love
programme.desc: When Emma is hired to bake for a royal cake, she finds herself at odds with a Prince.

programme.title: The Bouquet
programme.desc: Two estranged sisters set aside their differences to help their mother save her floral business.

programme.title: WWJD? The Journey Continues
programme.desc: WWJD continues as the drifter arrives into a new town , inspiring a group of people to live as Jesus would.

programme.title: A Father's Choice
programme.desc: An unforgettable story about the promise and the power of family.

programme.title: When Love Came To Town
programme.desc: Abby, an author is used to making up quirky, charming characters, but here in Bliss they have sprung to life.

programme.title: The Challenger
programme.desc: The Challenger follows the life struggles of a young, Bronx auto mechanic, Jaden Miller. When Jaden and his mother are evicted from their home, he soon finds that boxing may be the key to stop them from living on the streets and the ticket to a better life. With legendary trainer, Duane Taylor, Miller becomes a local hero and captures the attention of a cable network that decides to create a reality show around his goal to fight to the Light-Heavyweight Title.

programme.title: The Climb
programme.desc: The breath-taking story of two men ... one with a courageous heart who seeks God; the other with an independent spirit who relies on his own strength.

programme.title: Falcon Rising
programme.desc: Chapman is an ex-marine in Brazil's slums, battling the yakuza outfit who attacked his sister and left her for dead.

programme.title: The New Girl
programme.desc: When her sister Monica suddenly disappears under suspicious circumstances, Malorie becomes convinced that Monica's seemingly perfect husband is hiding something sinister. She then launches her own investigation and unearths a twisted web of lies, abuse, and even political conspiracy. Malorie must confront dangerous secrets that not only threaten her sister's life but her own.

programme.title: Take Back
programme.desc: Zara (Gillian White) and Brian (Michael Jai White) are living the perfect small town life when a mysterious stranger from Zara's secret past kidnaps their daughter and now they must race to save her life.

programme.title: But I'm Married
programme.desc: Kimora and Jamal, a successful power couple, face a dwindling passion in their marriage. Seeking to reignite their spark, Kimora explores unconventional options, including introducing a third person. This decision unleashes a whirlwind of hidden desires, lies, and betrayals. As secrets surface and trust erodes, Kimora must confront the consequences of her choices and rebuild her life, navigating the complexities of desire and the fragility of her marriage.

programme.title: Heart of a Woman
programme.desc: Alisha, a rising model with a bright future, chose to put her career on hold to stand by her football star husband Jax. When an opportunity arises for her to return to the runway, their marriage is put to the test, and whether love or career holds the most weight in their relationship.

programme.title: Somebody's Got to Die
programme.desc: After leading a life of contract killing, Jake makes a promise to his fiancé, Nicole, to go on the straight and narrow. He finds solace in running a bar with his brother Kelvin. When his former Kingpin Marcel offers him a way out, he is forced to confront shadows of his past, and overcome one of the biggest jobs of his criminal career.

programme.title: Guilty Victim
programme.desc: A dangerous investor with a violent past is pulled back into chaos when a home security scheme spirals out of control, forcing him to confront old wounds, betrayal, and a brutal fight to protect what little he has left.

programme.title: Independents' Day
programme.desc: Aliens invade Earth, tell humans to emigrate to a new planet or die. The President and a militia fight back.

programme.title: Asteroid Vs Earth
programme.desc: When meteors threaten Earth's extinction, its greatest minds devise a plan to tilt the planet off its axis.

programme.title: Super Cyclone
programme.desc: 300 MPH winds threaten America, so a lone meteorologist must battle the elements to stop the threat.

programme.title: Tomb Invader
programme.desc: Determined to find out why her mother disappeared, an adventurer enters the dangerous Tomb of Quin Shi Huang.

programme.title: American Warships
programme.desc: When a fleet of mysterious ships wage war against Earth, the crew of the USS Iowa must prevent armageddon.

programme.title: Ape vs. Monster
programme.desc: An ape in space crashes on Earth. Sludge from the ship triggers massive growth in the ape and a gila monster.

programme.title: The Prey (Edited Version)
programme.desc: Three young couples set off into the mountains for a weekend of climbing, drinking and lovemaking. But little do they know that they are stumbling into the terrain of a fearsome predator - a wild man, horrifically burned as a child many years ago in a fire which engulfed his gypsy camp and left only him alive. Now he roams the woods in search of his next human prey.

programme.title: Street Trash
programme.desc: In the year 2050, global economic turmoil has destroyed the middle class in Cape Town, South Africa, which is now divided between the ultra-rich and the displaced. Ronald and a group of fellow houseless friends uncover a plot by the local government to 'wash away' the houseless population with a sinister chemical agent called 'V.' Now, they must risk everything to expose the truth and resist a society thats determined to erase them.

programme.title: Mother's Day
programme.desc: When you go into the woods today, you're in for a big surprise. When you go into the woods today, you're not going to believe your eyes. But this ain't no Teddy Bear Picnic. Three women discover that two men are willing to do anything to impress Mother, and what impresses Mother is watching her sons commit acts of rape, torture and murder. Now these women are prisoners, lowered to the status of pawns in a game of chess waged between all three of them and two dimwits and their maniacal mommy; thus, the question becomes: can any of them escape from this game alive?

programme.title: The Island
programme.desc: The islanders of Sloborn, incuding newly pregnant 15-year-old Evelin, remain unaware of the deadly virus sweeping their island as they go about their busy lives.

programme.title: The Island
programme.desc: As Evelin struggles with her pregnancy, more and more people are succumbing to the sickness, the origin of which policeman Mikkel begins to question.

programme.title: Tales From The Void
programme.desc: Katies new pregnancy brings with it unforeseen complications that grow exponentially more horrifying in her uptight religious community.

programme.title: The Trouble With Tessa
programme.desc: Tessa returns from their overnight meeting to find that someone has been in their home...and stolen the tapes. As they attempt to come to terms with their new reality, Betsy drops in for afternoon tea and shares unsettling information that shifts the entire narrative around Amelias disappearance. Tessa begins to suspect that their privacy has been compromised in more ways than one, even as more bizarre disturbances emerge from the basement. What began as a search for answers has quickly become a descent into something far deeper.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: A struggling bookstore owner finds unpublished works of George Romero, but the comics lead to new troubles. Then, Shelby's mom struggles to accept that her baby is growing up, but hanging on to childhood relics isn't as harmless as she thinks.

programme.title: A Creepshow Animated Special
programme.desc: A surgeon crash-lands on a desert island ... how strong is his will to survive? Then, a teenager's family vacation becomes even more torturous when they pull off the road to see a sinister circus.

programme.title: A Creepshow Holiday Special
programme.desc: Robert Weston has reason to believe he might be a werewolf, but when he goes to a Shapeshifters Anonymous support group, he learns there's something far more sinister than his curse to worry about.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: After the death of a loved one, a drinking habit becomes insatiable. Then, dolls are Evie's favorite companions, but when a severed toy head appears in her dollhouse, Evie can no longer protect them.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: A platoon of American soldiers finds something more sinister than war behind enemy lines. Then, a man with a passion for found objects finds something particularly odd: a finger ... and it's growing.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Teens who go trick-or-treating are met with fear instead of smiles and sweets. Then, Justin brings home the wrong suitcase and is shocked to find a living man stuffed inside.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Harry stumbles upon an old scarecrow and brings it to life. Then, Lydia kills her lover and needs to hide the evidence, but after an earthquake she gets trapped inside an elevator with the body.

programme.title: Creepshow

Annie Oakley: Gail Davis, Deputy Craig: Brad Johnson, Tagg Oakley: Jimmy Hawkins. This western adventure series aired from 1954-1957. Cowboy star Gene Autrey was an executive producer for the show, which was nominated for a Primetime Emmy Award in 1955.

programme.title: Bad Man of Deadwood
programme.desc: Roy Rogers and Gabby Hayes are together again in this Republic Pictures gem. This time theyre trying to set up a medicine show in Deadwood, a town known for dangerous entrepreneurs who dont like competition. Sally Payne is on-hand as a member of the troop where Roy and Gabby work as entertainers, while Carol Adams plays a reporter who may have a romantic interest in Roy. Songs include Song of the Dusty Trail, and Home on the Rangeland.

programme.title: Leather Burners
programme.desc: Hopalong Cassidy (William Boyd) gets the call when a herd of rustled cattle mysteriously disappear. Andy Clyde, Victor Jory and George Reeves (televisions Superman!) also star in this, the 42nd of 66 Hopalong Cassidy features. If you look closely, you might even notice Robert Mitchum in an uncredited role!

programme.title: In Old Cheyenne
programme.desc: Roy Rogers plays a newspaper reporter sent to Cheyenne to investigate a reputed troublemaker. While on the scene, he discovers who the real bad guys are, and sets out to right some wrongs. Also starring Roy's usual sidekick George 'Gabby' Hayes, plus Joan Woodbury, and George Rosener. This Republic Pictures western was helmed by director Joseph Kane (The Undersea Kingdom, Dakota) and features two musical numbers: 'Bonita' and 'Linda Flor'.

programme.title: Northwest Trail
programme.desc: Mountie Matt OBrien assignment is simple: He is to escort the rather grouchy, and slightly annoying, Miss Katherine Owens to a remote outpost. programme.title: Daniel Boone
programme.desc: In 1775, Daniel Boone (George O'Brien) leads thirty colonial families to Kentucky where they face the threats of Native American marauders led by rebel Simon Girty (John Carradine). That's just the beginning, for there are even more dangers and clashes ahead for Boone and his party. With a cast that includes Ralph Forbes, Heather Angel, and lovable child star Dickie Jones, director David Howard takes us through one of the most exciting periods in American history!

programme.title: The Trail Beyond
programme.desc: Rod Drew (John Wayne) is in search of a missing miner and his daughter in this Lone Star Picture. Along the way, Drew rescues his pal Wabbi (Noah Beery Jr.) from card cheats who have framed him for murder. You'll find plenty of action and suspense in this vintage horse opera, originally distributed by the kings of the B western, Monogram Pictures!

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon opens Home Alone Week by making his ideal personal omelet ó the dish he truly loves cooking just for himself ó as the hosts celebrate an entire week of cooking without compromise, audience, or spousal interference. The conversation digs into the authentic food preferences everyone quietly keeps to themselves.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall stuns Michael and Clinton by revealing that her perfect home-alone meal is chili dogs ó a dish the Michelin-adjacent chef makes entirely for herself when no one is watching. The hosts celebrate the week's theme that authentic food preferences are often the most surprising and humanizing.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly wraps up Home Alone Week with the dish his husband Damon absolutely refuses to eat: a comforting Indian-inspired Tofu Sag Paneer riff that Clinton makes happily for himself when he has the kitchen to himself. The hosts celebrate the week's revelation that the most personal food choices are often the ones that define us most.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: A special compilation episode looking back at the best Chew It or Screw It moments from the past six months, featuring some of the show's most memorable guests playing along as the hosts ask them to weigh in on food, lifestyle, and totally random preferences. Segments include: Danielle Sepsy (the Scone Queen) forced to choose between scones and muffins—predictably choosing scones—plus her takes on stuffing vs. mashed potatoes and canned vs. homemade cranberry sauce; Liz Symon (Michael's wife) on orange wine, foofy cocktails, lazy sundaes, the New York Times Spelling Bee app (Queen Bee 99% of the time), stinky cheese, and the truth about cuddling with Michael; Marysol Castro on Citi Field hot dogs, pineapple on pizza (a hard Bronx no), and celebrity cameos at sporting events (the exception being Bad Bunny); Amanda Freitag and the crew on going out to eat on Valentine's Day (screw it—you get the worst service and cooks who rarely go out); Devyn Simone on cooking for someone on a first date (surprisingly chew it, as long as boundaries are established) and not texting the next day after getting lucky; Esther Choi on breakfast for dinner, ketchup on eggs (a unanimous screw it from the entire panel); Scott Conant on sending food back at a restaurant (screw it—you're an adult, figure it out), family style with strangers (chew it), and splitting the check 12 ways; Damaris Phillips on cornbread with sugar, Miracle Whip with bologna, and the boat-ride romance that made her appreciate Miracle Whip on a sandwich; Maneet Chauhan on spicy desserts, taking the last bite when sharing a dish (chew it if you're single and looking for a partner), and eating off someone else's plate; Mika Leon on the five-second rule, helicopter parenting, staying up 24 hours to cook (chew it—she did 32-plus hours on a cooking competition), and going to the club (hard pass, she's tired); and Chef Jackie Bula on air fryers (screw it—just fry), and reality cooking competitions (screw it—it's less about the food than the show). Audience engagement prompts woven throughout invite viewers to comment on their own chew-it-or-screw-it takes.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: The Chewed Up crew sets up poolside at the W Hotel South Beach for a breezy St. Patrick's Day episode that swaps corned beef for Cuban grilled shrimp. Michael Symon, whose Cleveland neighborhood is deeply Irish, admits he's been celebrating St. Patrick's Day with his best friend Jimmy for decades, and this year Jimmy joins them in Miami. Michael's Grilled Shrimp Mojo is a tribute to the Cuban flavors of Miami: shrimp marinated in lime zest, lime juice, chili flakes, and olive oil, then grilled and finished with a vibrant mojo sauce blended from sour orange, onion, a whole head of garlic, cilantro, parsley, and oregano. The key tip: cook shrimp until they look like commas, not O'sóthe moment they curl completely is the moment they're overdone. Clinton reflects on his seven years living in Miami and the city's culinary depth, from Cuban staples to modern restaurants like Yardbird. Carla raises her fear of sharks; Clinton counters with his inexplicable terror of birds getting caught in his hair. Michael confesses he bought a bathing suit specifically to go to the beach, put it on, saw himself in the mirror, and decided to spend the afternoon in bed instead.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Just in time for National Ravioli Day, acclaimed chef and Chopped judge Scott Conant joins the trio at the W South Beach to make his delicate ricotta gnocchiótechnically gnudióalongside his signature Martone Street jarred tomato sauce. Michael has been close friends with Scott for 15 years, and Clinton admits he was genuinely nervous before meeting the notoriously stern Chopped judge, quickly discovering he's warm and funny in person. Scott explains that Martone Street is named for his grandparents' home in Waterbury, Connecticut, where Sunday dinners taught him that conversation over food is one of life's greatest gifts. The gnocchi dough combines fresh ricotta, basil, breadcrumbs, flour, egg, and Parmesan, shaped into small dumplings and cooked until they float. Scott builds a stabilized butter sauce using pasta water and cornstarch as an emulsifieróa trick that keeps it smooth for hours. Finished with Martone Street sauce and broccoli rabe, the result is stunning in its simplicity. His philosophy runs throughout: spaghetti aglio e olio, done correctly, is one of the most complex and satisfying dishes imaginable. Scott becomes emotional talking about watching his 16-year-old daughter drive for the first time and the joy of steak cook-offs with his girls at home.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Food Network Star winner and Southern food authority Damaris Phillips brings a taste of Louisville poolside at the W South Beach, demonstrating three Southern tea sandwich classics that are anything but fussy. Carla Hall has been singing Damaris's praises for yearsómutual friend Jackie Guido insisted they were destined to be peas and carrotsóand the episode proves her right, the two radiating warmth and joy throughout. Damaris makes Pimento Cheese; Benedictine, a Louisville specialty of grated cucumber with dill and cream cheese (the key being squeezing out all the cucumber water first); and Ham Salad, essentially chicken salad's porky cousin, versatile as either sweet or savory. Everything is layered on Japanese milk bread or thin white bread, crusts removed, and assembled into dainty finger sandwiches and triple-decker stacks. Michael immediately tries to build an enormous open-face sandwich rather than the delicate bites Damaris intended, sparking a lively debate about proportion and restraint. Carla shares that she recently spent a cozy Super Sunday with Damaris in Louisvilleóa beloved tradition of soups, board games, and community. The episode also features a spirited group effort to talk Damaris out of her plan to get a leopard-print face tattoo, with limited success. The episode ends with genuine laughter, warm hugs, and unanimous agreement: the sandwiches are delicious.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Two-time Tournament of Champions winner and Chopped veteran Maneet Chauhan arrives at the W South Beach as a true Queen of Flavor, demonstrating her Indian Spiced Ceviche, a dish that applies Indian culinary logic to coastal Latin technique. Fresh fish is cured in lime juice in the traditional ceviche manner, then transformed with coconut milk to soften the heat and balance the citrus, and finished with a tarka: the Indian technique of blooming whole spices in searing hot coconut oil and pouring it directly over the dish at the last second. The result is layered, bright, and unexpected. Maneet explains that she grew up in a small town in India and learned to cook by exploring her neighbors' kitchens, each representing a different regional tradition. She recounts a pivotal story from Peru, where she made potato pancakes alongside a local woman without sharing a single word of common languageóconnected entirely through food and gesture. That philosophy runs through her Nashville restaurant, which introduces diners to bold Indian flavors through fusion dishes like hot chicken korma and chicken tikka salad poutine. She draws a parallel between the tarka and the French beurre blancóboth finishing techniques that transform a dish in the final seconds of cooking. Michael, Clinton, and Carla taste the ceviche with genuine surprise and awe.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: As the Chewed Up crew wraps up their week at the W South Beach, Miami-born chef Mika Leon brings the most authentically local dish of the entire trip: Pan Con Lechon, the slow-roasted Cuban pork sandwich that anchors Noche Buena, Cuban Christmas Eve. Mika woke up at 4 AM to start the pork shoulderómarinated overnight in a mojo sauce built on sour orange juice, lime juice, orange juice, and 50 cloves of garlic from her grandfather's recipe. After six hours at 350 degrees, the pork is shredded and crisped with additional mojo in a hot pan, then piled high on a special Cuban rollósweeter and fattier than regular bread, made with lard throughout. Mika shares her journey from dabbling in TV production (she quit after a week) to opening a food truck selling $5 Pan Con Lechons at 3 AM to Miami club-goers, which grew into a full restaurant and now a cookbook. Carla notes that the best cooks often learn from family before any formal training, and this dish proves it. The group discusses Miami's cultural identityómore its own country than a Florida cityódefined by Spanglish, its own traffic logic, and immigrant food traditions passed down for generations. Clinton finally admits that despite a full week at the W, none of them ever made it into the pool.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: The South Beach week closes with a live audience tapingóthe most unhinged episode of the tripóas Michael Symon, Carla Hall, and Clinton Kelly perform in front of a crowd of fans. Michael's voice is noticeably shot from a long week of celebrating, so Clinton and Carla carry extra energy while he freestyle hosts from notes he promptly loses within 30 seconds. Carla makes her refreshing Pineapple Lime Agua Fresca: blended fresh pineapple with lime water (lime juice diluted to avoid sharpness), ginger, honey, and wateróa batched mocktail or cocktail mixer that's as beautiful as it is easy. She shares her tip for getting full use of citrus by zesting before juicing. The episode features 'Chew It or Screw It,' a voting game where the audience weighs in on Florida's most adventurous foods: iguana pizza, python sausage, alligator ribs, conch fritters, and fried frog legs. Carla and Clinton report on their spontaneous 7 AM sunrise bike rideótheir first ever togetheróand Clinton jokes about his impossibly pale, non-tanning legs. The trio explores Florida's wildlife peculiarities, from iguanas falling from trees in cold snaps to flying cockroaches given affectionate nicknames. A Chewed Up Challenge heads-up guessing game rounds out the audience interaction with giveaways. Season 3's Miami week ends exactly how it began: loud, funny, and hungry.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Season 3 returns to the studio with a spring episode that puts handmade pasta front and center. Michael Symon makes his grandmother's Cavatellióa ricotta-based pasta dough (1 cup flour, 1 egg, 1.5 cups ricotta, Parmesan) that is more forgiving than traditional egg pasta, closer in spirit to a dumpling than a proper noodle. He demonstrates the technique his grandmother taught him as a child: roll the dough into thin strands, cut into inch-long pieces, and press-roll each one with a butter knife to create a hollow, ridged shape that opens slightly when cooked and catches every drop of sauce. Carla has a revelation mid-episode realizing cavatelli is essentially a dumplingóthe Italian version of a global tradition that spans every culture. The sauce is spring itself: brown butter carried almost to the edge, blanched peas, bias-cut asparagus, pasta water, and lemon. Michael also reflects on the memory of cooking as familyópasta making as a group project, a Sunday activity with kids or grandkids, something imperfect that matters anyway. The episode weaves in conversations about spring weather chaos on the East Coast, Gen X collectively aging past the Golden Girls cast, Michael's habit of texting Cleveland friends his Venice Beach weather reports, and a team-meeting story where Nick's extended hand produced entirely different reactions from all three hosts based on their respective cultural backgrounds. Nick debuts a new segment, 'Choose Wisely,' a spring-edition 'most likely to' game. Sponsored by Fresh Direct and Front Gate.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Top Chef alum and Carla Hall's culinary producer Ariane Duarte joins the Chewed Up crew to make spring crostini three ways on homemade sourdough focaccia, while the hosts debate food rules and play Chew It or Screw It.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Cooking Show
programme.desc: "Dinner with Tyler Huggins" - Our guest this week is Tyler Huggins, founder and CEO of Meati. Dave has prepared a worldwide tour of bar foods that includes “pizza okonomiyaki,” hwe dup bap, galbijjim, and Korean army stew.

programme.title: Majordomo Cookbook Club
programme.desc: Majordomo Cookbook Club - Caramelized Milk Chocolate with Plantain Brittle and Sticky Rice & Mango Ice Cream: Adrienne Borlongan of Wanderlust Creamery—one of the most popular (and buzziest) ice cream chains in LA—stopped by the studio to show how she makes two different ice creams: Caramelized Milk Chocolate with Plantain Brittle, and the flavor that put Wanderlust on the map—Sticky Rice & Mango!

programme.title: Majordomo Cookbook Club
programme.desc: Majordomo Cookbook Club - Fried Kingfish Collar with Homemade Tartar Sauce: Australian chef (and master fish butcher) Josh Niland makes fried Kingfish collar with a zesty homemade tartar sauce.

programme.title: Majordomo Cookbook Club
programme.desc: Majordomo Cookbook Club - Korean "Drug Eggs" Extremely Addicting Soy Sauce-Marinated Eggs with Rice (Mayak Gyeranbap): Deuki Hong and Matt Rodbard, authors of Koreaworld: A Cookbook, make Mayak Gyeranbap! These sweet, garlicky, soy-infused eggs can be loosely translated as "drug eggs," so named because of their magnetic craveability, a quality they share with their spiritual brethren, Taiwanese tea eggs. The eggs are cooked to a soft and yolky consistency and served over rice as a full meal or by themselves for a robust snack.

programme.title: Majordomo Cookbook Club
programme.desc: Majordomo Cookbook Club - Priya Krishna's Dahi Bhalla & Shortcut Profiteroles: New York Times food reporter and bestselling cookbook author Priya Krishna came by the studio and prepared two recipes from her new cookbook for kids, Priya's Kitchen Adventures. She makes shortcut profiteroles inspired by her time in Paris, plus dahi bhalla, essentially fritters soaked in yogurt and topped with all kinds of chutneys. Yum!

programme.title: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes
programme.desc: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes - Chilaquiles; Warm Artichoke Salad; Ants Climbing a Tree - Andrew makes a delcious Mexican breakfast classic, chilaquiles, and a simple warm artichoke salad, then Chris joins him in the kitchen to make "Ants Climbing a Tree" (Ma Yi Shang Shu), a classic Sichuan dish consisting of glass noodles in a delicious sauce with ground pork and chopped scallions.

programme.title: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes
programme.desc: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes - Pepperoni Loaf Pimento Grilled Cheese & Everything-Spice Flatbread: Andrew makes Christina Tosi's ultra-indulgent pepperoni loaf, then combines it with David Chang's pimento cheese to put together a crazy grilled cheese sandwich; Andrew recreates David Chang's everything-spiced flatbread—a recipe that started with Chinese bing dough, took a stop in Istanbul, and ended in a New York deli. It's flaky, buttery and delicious!

programme.title: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes
programme.desc: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes - Oyakodon & Mortadella Sandwich: Chris Ying cooks a Japanese dish called Oyakodon that he made for his buddy David Chang on a recent episode of Dinner Time Live. Oyakodon translates to “parent and child bowl” and refers to the main ingredients: chicken (the parent) and egg (the child). Chicken thighs and sliced onions are simmered in seasoned dashi, then covered in egg, which forms a custard-y blanket to be served over rice. Chris and Andrew make a Dinner Time Live recipe based on one of David Chang's favorite sandwiches in the world: a cheesy, hot, mortadella sandwich. This version has a twist—a Chinese steamed bun as a tribute to Momofuku's famous pork buns.

programme.title: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes
programme.desc: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes - Pan Roasted Salmon with Caper Butter Sauce, Green Beans & Potato Rösti - Andrew prepares another delicious meal from the Classic Comforts menu of Dinner Time Live: Pan roasted, dry aged salmon with a caper butter sauce, and a buttery potato rösti, basically a fancy-sounding Swiss-German term for hash browns.

programme.title: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes
programme.desc: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes - Clam Chowder & Spaghetti with Clams - Andrew prepares two recipes from Dinner Time Live: A clam chowder so cozy and good that New Englanders would approve, and Spaghetti with Clams—a simple and delicious pasta dish.

programme.title: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes
programme.desc: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes - Lemongrass Beef Skewers, Bánh Mì Tea Sandwiches, Spicy Beef Stew - Chris and Andrew make three different dishes from the Seven Courses of Beef episode of Dinner Time Live, including an interpretation of Vietnamese Bo Kho (a spicy beef stew) and Bánh Mì reimagined as a dainty tea sandwich.

programme.desc: Little Italy: When I was growing up, a trip to Baltimore’s Little Italy always meant culinary adventure. Today we celebrate Italian grilling and the food of Little Italys around North America, honoring the Italian reverence for simplicity and fresh seasonal ingredients, and the American passion for big flavors and ingenious grilling techniques.

programme.title: Sara's Weeknight Meals
programme.desc: Holidays are when Sara pulls out the stops when it comes to sweets and desserts. Try her Glazed Pears and shortbread pecan praline, or the surprising twist on potstickers filled with dried plum and apricots. On Ask Sara, tips on blind baking a pie crust. Then a favorite food gift in Sara's family - homemade Chocolate Peppermint Bark.

programme.title: Sara's Weeknight Meals
programme.desc: Chicken or eggs? Sara's got some amazing recipes to make them shine, starting with a chicken paillard, slathered in a compound date butter. Baked Chicken thighs are pointed up with the acid of homemade lemon pickles. Then a French chef shows Sara a simple galette of ham and eggs wrapped in pastry - perfect for breakfast, lunch or dinner.

programme.title: Sara's Weeknight Meals
programme.desc: Sara's on the Spanish island of Mallorca, where an ancient olive grove tended by sheep is the magical setting for cooking a hunters stew over an open fire. Back home, Sara makes Seafood Gazpacho. On 'Ask Sara', she answers a viewer's questions about Lemons.

programme.title: Sara's Weeknight Meals
programme.desc: Fish is on the menu when Sara joins Greek Chef Maria Loi harborside near Athens to cook lemony, crispy skinned Branzino along with Beet, Beans and Feta Salad and Lemon Potatoes. Then it's off to Athen's Central Market to buy Retsina at a famous tavern for a rooftop mezze party in the shadow of the Acropolis. Back at home, Sara uses canned sardines for a quick and easy pantry taco.

programme.title: Sara's Weeknight Meals
programme.desc: Sara visits the romantic Greek island of Santorini to sip local wine on the edge of the Caldera. Then she joins a local chef to make Greek salad, Tzatziki and Shrimp Saganaki. In Vancouver, Sara learns their secrets of the coveted Spot Prawn from a Vancouver fisherman, then joins a friend to make Spot Prawn Risotto.

programme.title: Sara's Weeknight Meals
programme.desc: A food tour of Tunisia's Sidi Bou Said with local chef Malek Labidi ends cooking Shakshuka in a beautiful garden overlooking the sea. Later we dive into the Medina in Tunis to watch women hand make couscous. In Istanbul, a shopping trip with local TV chef Refika Burgul, ends in the kitchen making Salt Cured Sea Bass plus the addictive Turkish beef and French fry favorite, Kochertme.

programme.title: Lidia's Kitchen
programme.desc: Lidia creates a delicious meal of celery stalks stuffed with gorgonzola and apples; lamb ragu prepared with an array of peppers and potatoes; and a gianduia cake made of hazelnuts and chocolate-a Piedmontese tradition.

programme.title: Lidia's Kitchen
programme.desc: Today Lidia's menu includes: celery, artichoke and mortadella salad, "crespelle manicotti" stuffed with ricotta and mozzarella, and for dessert, an Italian classic - beautiful strawberries topped with balsamic vinegar.

programme.title: Lidia's Kitchen

programme.title: Lidia's Kitchen

programme.title: Lidia's Kitchen
programme.desc: Lidia's first dish is a velvety lettuce soup in a fontina gratin; she then makes a juicy pork tenderloin with balsamic onions. Lastly, she prepares string beans with rocky mashed potatoes made with garlic and olive oil.

programme.title: Lidia's Kitchen
programme.desc: Lidia makes an orecchiette with clams and zucchini. Her spaghetti Caruso is made with a hearty sauce of mixed mushrooms, tomatoes, peas and chicken livers.

programme.title: Lidia's Kitchen

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: The O'Kane family's patience is truly tested during a complete to-the-studs kitchen renovation. Homeowner Yvonne starts to get impatient to feed her family home cooked dinners. Added pressure: another fireplace to deal with, uneven floors, custom cabinets and an uncovered secret staircase. (All that plus a new puppy.) Thanks to Mar's 6 design principles this custom space is unique and stunning.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: It's 'Lost in Space' because somehow these home makeover clips never made it to Mar's! Bloopers Alert! Unseen Tips Alert! Inspired by viewer feedback, an entire show that includes highlights of the greatest gaffs, goofs and guffaws. Plus, some renovation and makeover projects never before aired.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Mar Jennings uses his 6 Casual Luxury design principles to transform a typical master bedroom, closets and bath into a boutique, hotel-style oasis. The O'Kanes are desperate to stop sleeping on their couches and get back into their master bedroom - but with a new cathedral ceiling installation, private deck installation, custom vanities, several setbacks and delays this renovation won't be easy.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Not only does Mar Jennings have the task of pleasing the adult O'Kanes, but he has to satisfy Teddy, a young man between childhood and young adulthood. AND try to make space in an already-small bedroom. Can it even be done? All while juggling two desk and office spaces-for Teddy and his parents. Mar's unexpected solutions for this kids bedroom and office makeover are truly Casual Luxury.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: More things that regular episodes just couldn't fit in, so we say they didn't quite make it to Mar's! Filled with some of Mar's favorite people and places that viewers wanted to see more of, and of course some delightfully hilarious bloopers, too. Plus, more renovation, makeover and design sMARt tips (like fixing uneven floors) and great locations. As always, Casual Luxury has no boundaries.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Mar is tasked with carving out two new basement rooms from storage space: a studio and laundry room. With Mar's 6 design principles, he must make dark and dank become bright and stunning. The homeowners expect to be wowed by these 'creative' and 'clean' space makeovers, but with pipes and beams in the way and unexpected challenges, this project might just push Mar over the edge.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Any home makeover has its ups and downs, but it's literally true with this staircase and foyer. Mar renovates the home's core areas, illustrating how to reclaim needed space, while creating impactful design that doesn't compete with adjacent rooms. An unexpected find-a gorgeous chandelier from a famous NY building-will be the perfect finishing touch if it makes it through the tricky installation.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: The Big Finale features Mar designing two useful, decorative his-and-hers structures to grace the pool area. Sheds become elevated design when form follows function--and include Mar's six core principles of Casual Luxury. Mar and his pup Violet find time for a much-needed minibreak to pet-friendly locations and shopping in NYC right before the final makeover reveal delights with surprise guests.

programme.title: Dream of Italy
